Section: #Architecture
The system consists of three core layers: an Identity Provider (SPIFFE/SPIRE) that issues short-lived SVIDs to workloads, a Policy Engine that evaluates access requests against fine-grained OPA policies, and an Envoy-based Data Plane that enforces mTLS and authorization at the network level.

Section: #Usage
Configure workload identities and access policies in YAML. The framework automatically provisions SPIFFE identities and enforces mTLS between services.

Section: #Performance
The framework adds less than 2ms latency per request for mTLS handshake and policy evaluation. Tested with 10,000 concurrent connections across 50 microservices.

Section: #Projects
##Zero-Trust Network Architecture (CyberArk)

Designed and implemented a zero-trust segmentation framework for enterprise identity platforms. The system enforces least-privilege access across 50+ microservices using mutual TLS and SPIFFE/SPIRE identity. Reduced lateral movement attack surface by 60%. Technologies: Go, Envoy Proxy, SPIFFE, Kubernetes, Terraform.

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to recommend Dana Levy, who has been a key member of my security research team at CyberArk for the past four years. Dana joined us as a security engineer and quickly distinguished herself through exceptional technical depth and leadership ability.

Dana's most impactful contribution has been the design and implementation of our zero-trust network architecture framework. She led a team of 4 engineers to build a SPIFFE/SPIRE-based identity system that reduced our lateral movement attack surface by 60%. Section: #Architecture
The system consists of three core layers: an Identity Provider (SPIFFE/SPIRE) that issues short-lived SVIDs to workloads, a Policy Engine that evaluates access requests against fine-grained OPA policies, and an Envoy-based Data Plane that enforces mTLS and authorization at the network level.

Section: #Usage
Configure workload identities and access policies in YAML. The framework automatically provisions SPIFFE identities and enforces mTLS between services.

Section: #Overview
This framework enforces least-privilege access across microservice architectures using mutual TLS, SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity, and dynamic policy enforcement. Designed for enterprise environments with 50+ microservices requiring strict identity verification for every network request.

Section: #Architecture
The system consists of three core layers: an Identity Provider (SPIFFE/SPIRE) that issues short-lived SVIDs to workloads, a Policy Engine that evaluates access requests against fine-grained OPA policies, and an Envoy-based Data Plane that enforces mTLS and authorization at the network level.

Section: From: Dr. Alon Kaufman, VP of Security Research at CyberArk
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to recommend Dana Levy, who has been a key member of my security research team at CyberArk for the past four years. Dana joined us as a security engineer and quickly distinguished herself through exceptional technical depth and leadership ability.

Dana's most impactful contribution has been the design and implementation of our zero-trust network architecture framework. She led a team of 4 engineers to build a SPIFFE/SPIRE-based identity system that reduced our lateral movement attack surface by 60%. This project demonstrated her ability to combine deep security knowledge with practical engineering excellence.

Her work on the automated threat intelligence platform has been equally impressive. Dana architected a system that ingests over 500,000 indicators of compromise daily, dramatically improving our threat detection capabilities. She also built our internal red team automation framework, which has been used in 20+ enterprise client engagements.

Section: #Architecture
The system consists of three core layers: an Identity Provider (SPIFFE/SPIRE) that issues short-lived SVIDs to workloads, a Policy Engine that evaluates access requests against fine-grained OPA policies, and an Envoy-based Data Plane that enforces mTLS and authorization at the network level.

Section: ##Automated Threat Intelligence Platform (CyberArk)
Built an end-to-end threat intelligence aggregation platform ingesting 500K+ indicators of compromise daily from OSINT feeds, commercial threat intel, and internal honeypots. Automated enrichment and correlation with Splunk SIEM. Technologies: Python, Apache Kafka, Elasticsearch, Splunk, Docker.

Section: #Architecture
The system consists of three core layers: an Identity Provider (SPIFFE/SPIRE) that issues short-lived SVIDs to workloads, a Policy Engine that evaluates access requests against fine-grained OPA policies, and an Envoy-based Data Plane that enforces mTLS and authorization at the network level.

Section: #Performance
The framework adds less than 2ms latency per request for mTLS handshake and policy evaluation. Tested with 10,000 concurrent connections across 50 microservices.

Section: #Overview
This framework enforces least-privilege access across microservice architectures using mutual TLS, SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity, and dynamic policy enforcement. Designed for enterprise environments with 50+ microservices requiring strict identity verification for every network request.

Section: ##Academic Thesis: Adversarial ML on IDS
Developed novel evasion techniques against ML-based network intrusion detection systems using GANs. Demonstrated 85% evasion rate against state-of-the-art models while maintaining traffic validity. Published in IEEE S&P Workshop. Technologies: Python, PyTorch, Scapy.

Section: #Summary
Senior Security Engineer with 5 years of experience in offensive security, threat hunting, and zero-trust architecture design. Passionate about proactive defense and open-source security research.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Yoni Abergel

Section: Overview
This framework enables organizations to migrate workloads between AWS, GCP, and Azure with automated service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting, and instant rollback capabilities. Built for Monday.com's migration of 30+ services to a hybrid cloud architecture.

Section: Certifications
1. AWS Certified Solutions Architect — Professional (February 2022, Amazon Web Services)

2. Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) (August 2021, CNCF)

3. HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate (May 2020, HashiCorp)

Section: DevOps Engineer — Wix.com
August 2019 – March 2021 | Tel Aviv, Israel

Managed AWS infrastructure for Wix's editor platform serving millions of users.

Built automated infrastructure provisioning using Terraform and Ansible for 100+ microservices.

Developed custom Jenkins pipelines for continuous integration across 50+ repositories.

Implemented centralized logging with ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) processing 5TB+ logs daily.

Reduced infrastructure provisioning time from 2 days to 15 minutes through automation.

Collaborated with security team to implement automated vulnerability scanning in CI pipelines.

Section: Tech Stack
Language: Go, Python

Infrastructure: Terraform, Kubernetes

Service Mesh: Istio for traffic management

Monitoring: Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog

CI/CD: ArgoCD, GitHub Actions

Cloud: AWS (EKS, S3, RDS), GCP (GKE, Cloud SQL)

Section: Self-Service Deployment Platform (Monday.com)
Built a developer portal enabling 150+ engineers to deploy, rollback, and monitor services independently. Features automated canary releases, feature flags, and deployment health checks. Reduced deployment lead time from 4 hours to 15 minutes. Technologies: Go, React, ArgoCD, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL.

Section: Usage
Define migration plans in YAML specifying source and target clusters, traffic shifting strategy (canary/blue-green), and health check criteria. The controller progressively shifts traffic while monitoring error rates and latency.

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: Interests
Contributing to CNCF open-source projects

Writing technical blog posts about Kubernetes and cloud architecture

Home lab enthusiast (self-hosted infrastructure)

Mountain biking and surfing

Section: Academic: Auto-Scaling Prediction System
Capstone project building an ML-based Kubernetes autoscaler that predicts traffic patterns using LSTM networks and pre-scales pods accordingly. Reduced over-provisioning by 30% in test environments. Technologies: Python, TensorFlow, Kubernetes, Prometheus.

Section: Performance
Successfully migrated 30+ services with zero downtime. Average migration time per service: 2 hours including validation. Supports clusters with up to 2,000 pods.
ground_truth: DevOps Engineer at Wix.com (August 2019 – March 2021).

Section: Overview
This framework enables organizations to migrate workloads between AWS, GCP, and Azure with automated service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting, and instant rollback capabilities. Built for Monday.com's migration of 30+ services to a hybrid cloud architecture.

Section: Senior DevOps Engineer — Monday.com
April 2021 – Present | Tel Aviv, Israel

Architecting multi-cloud infrastructure (AWS + GCP) serving 200K+ daily active users.

Leading a platform engineering team of 5, establishing SRE practices and on-call rotations.

Built comprehensive observability stack with Prometheus, Grafana, and Datadog covering 500+ services.

Section: Usage
Define migration plans in YAML specifying source and target clusters, traffic shifting strategy (canary/blue-green), and health check criteria. The controller progressively shifts traffic while monitoring error rates and latency.

Section: Self-Service Deployment Platform (Monday.com)
Built a developer portal enabling 150+ engineers to deploy, rollback, and monitor services independently. Features automated canary releases, feature flags, and deployment health checks. Reduced deployment lead time from 4 hours to 15 minutes. Technologies: Go, React, ArgoCD, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL.

Section: Open-Source: TerraLint
Created an open-source Terraform linting and policy enforcement tool that validates IaC against organizational best practices. Supports custom rules, cost estimation, and security checks. 150+ stars on GitHub. Technologies: Go, HCL parser.

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: Overview
This framework enables organizations to migrate workloads between AWS, GCP, and Azure with automated service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting, and instant rollback capabilities. Built for Monday.com's migration of 30+ services to a hybrid cloud architecture.

Section: Tech Stack
Language: Go, Python

Infrastructure: Terraform, Kubernetes

Service Mesh: Istio for traffic management

Monitoring: Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog

CI/CD: ArgoCD, GitHub Actions

Cloud: AWS (EKS, S3, RDS), GCP (GKE, Cloud SQL)

Section: Technical Skills
Programming Languages: Go, Python, Bash, TypeScript, HCL

Cloud Platforms: AWS (EKS, EC2, S3, Lambda, RDS, CloudFront, Route53), GCP (GKE, Cloud Run, BigQuery, Cloud Functions)

Container Orchestration: Kubernetes, Docker, Helm, Kustomize, Istio

Infrastructure as Code: Terraform, Pulumi, Ansible, CloudFormation

CI/CD: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, ArgoCD, Spinnaker

Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, ELK Stack, Jaeger, PagerDuty

Databases: PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB, DynamoDB

Networking & Security: Nginx, HAProxy, Cloudflare, HashiCorp Vault, cert-manager

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: Self-Service Deployment Platform (Monday.com)
Built a developer portal enabling 150+ engineers to deploy, rollback, and monitor services independently. Features automated canary releases, feature flags, and deployment health checks. Reduced deployment lead time from 4 hours to 15 minutes. Technologies: Go, React, ArgoCD, Kubernetes, PostgreSQL.

Section: Usage
Define migration plans in YAML specifying source and target clusters, traffic shifting strategy (canary/blue-green), and health check criteria. The controller progressively shifts traffic while monitoring error rates and latency.

Section: Interests
Contributing to CNCF open-source projects

Writing technical blog posts about Kubernetes and cloud architecture

Home lab enthusiast (self-hosted infrastructure)

Mountain biking and surfing

Section: Open-Source: TerraLint
Created an open-source Terraform linting and policy enforcement tool that validates IaC against organizational best practices. Supports custom rules, cost estimation, and security checks. 150+ stars on GitHub. Technologies: Go, HCL parser.

Section: Multi-Cloud Migration Framework (Monday.com)
Designed a framework for migrating 30+ services from single-cloud AWS to hybrid AWS/GCP architecture. Includes automated service dependency mapping, traffic shifting with Istio, and rollback capabilities. Reduced cloud costs by 25% through workload optimization. Technologies: Terraform, Kubernetes, Istio, Go.

Section: Multi-Cloud Migration Framework
Automated framework for migrating microservices across cloud providers with zero downtime.

question: What networking and security tools does the candidate know?
answer:
The candidate is proficient with the following **networking and security tools**, as detailed in their documents:

### **Networking Tools**  
- **Nginx**: Used for load balancing and web serving.  
- **HAProxy**: Employed for TCP/HTTP load balancing.  
- **Cloudflare**: Leveraged for DNS and DDoS protection.  
- **Kubernetes Networking**: Utilized Istio for service mesh and traffic management.  
- **Terraform**: Used to provision cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP) with networking components.  

### **Security Tools**  
- **HashiCorp Vault**: Integrated for secret management and secure credential storage.  
- **cert-manager**: Managed TLS certificate automation for Kubernetes services.  
- **Security Scanning**: Automated vulnerability scanning in CI/CD pipelines (e.g., Wix.com role).  
- **Terraform Security Checks**: Built-in policies and rules in TerraLint (open-source tool) for IaC security.  

### **Additional Context**  
- **Observability**: Tools like **Prometheus**, **Grafana**, and **Datadog** were used for monitoring system health and detecting anomalies (security-related).  
- **CI/CD Security**: Implemented security checks in pipelines (e.g., Wix.com role).  

These tools were applied in roles at **Wix.com** and **Monday.com**, focusing on infrastructure automation, multi-cloud security, and observability.

question: Tell me about the multi-cloud migration project.
answer:
The multi-cloud migration project involved designing and implementing a framework to migrate 30+ services from a single-cloud AWS environment to a hybrid AWS/GCP architecture. Key aspects include:  

- **Framework Development**: Automated tools for service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting (using Istio), and instant rollback capabilities to ensure zero downtime during migrations.  
- **Cost Optimization**: Achieved a **25% reduction in cloud costs** through workload optimization and efficient resource allocation.  
- **Technologies**: Leveraged **Terraform**, **Kubernetes (EKS/GKE)**, **Istio**, and **Go** for infrastructure automation, orchestration, and microservices management.  
- **Scalability**: Supported clusters with up to 2,000 pods and enabled self-service deployment for 150+ developers via automated canary releases.  
- **Team Leadership**: Led a platform engineering team of 5, establishing SRE practices and improving CI/CD efficiency by 60% through parallelization and caching.  

The project was highlighted in a Letter of Recommendation as a transformative initiative, showcasing the candidate’s ability to execute large-scale infrastructure changes while maintaining system reliability and cost efficiency.

Section: Kubernetes Custom Autoscaler (Monday.com)

Section: DevOps Engineer — Wix.com
August 2019 – March 2021 | Tel Aviv, Israel

Managed AWS infrastructure for Wix's editor platform serving millions of users.

Built automated infrastructure provisioning using Terraform and Ansible for 100+ microservices.

Developed custom Jenkins pipelines for continuous integration across 50+ repositories.

Implemented centralized logging with ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) processing 5TB+ logs daily.

Reduced infrastructure provisioning time from 2 days to 15 minutes through automation.

Collaborated with security team to implement automated vulnerability scanning in CI pipelines.

Section: Usage
Define migration plans in YAML specifying source and target clusters, traffic shifting strategy (canary/blue-green), and health check criteria. The controller progressively shifts traffic while monitoring error rates and latency.

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: Overview
This framework enables organizations to migrate workloads between AWS, GCP, and Azure with automated service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting, and instant rollback capabilities. Built for Monday.com's migration of 30+ services to a hybrid cloud architecture.

Section: Interests
Contributing to CNCF open-source projects

Writing technical blog posts about Kubernetes and cloud architecture

Home lab enthusiast (self-hosted infrastructure)

Mountain biking and surfing

Section: Key Features
Automated service dependency discovery and mapping

Progressive traffic shifting with configurable canary percentages

Real-time health monitoring with automatic rollback on degradation

Multi-cloud cost optimization and workload placement

Terraform state migration between cloud providers

25% cloud cost reduction through workload optimization

Section: Performance
Successfully migrated 30+ services with zero downtime. Average migration time per service: 2 hours including validation. Supports clusters with up to 2,000 pods.

Section: Multi-Cloud Migration Framework
Automated framework for migrating microservices across cloud providers with zero downtime.

Capstone project building an ML-based Kubernetes autoscaler that predicts traffic patterns using LSTM networks and pre-scales pods accordingly. Reduced over-provisioning by 30% in test environments. Technologies: Python, TensorFlow, Kubernetes, Prometheus.

Section: Usage
Define migration plans in YAML specifying source and target clusters, traffic shifting strategy (canary/blue-green), and health check criteria. The controller progressively shifts traffic while monitoring error rates and latency.

Section: Architecture
The framework consists of three components: a Discovery Agent that maps service dependencies and data flows, a Migration Controller that orchestrates progressive traffic shifting via Istio service mesh, and a Validation Engine that continuously verifies service health during migration.

Section: Overview
This framework enables organizations to migrate workloads between AWS, GCP, and Azure with automated service dependency mapping, progressive traffic shifting, and instant rollback capabilities. Built for Monday.com's migration of 30+ services to a hybrid cloud architecture.

I am delighted to recommend Maya Chen, who has been an exceptional member of our frontend engineering team at Fiverr for the past three years. Maya joined us straight from university and has rapidly become one of our most impactful engineers.

60+ fully accessible React components

Design token system with light and dark themes

Full WCAG 2.1 AA compliance

40% reduction in UI development time

Section: #Architecture
The system uses a monorepo architecture with Turborepo, organizing components into three tiers: Primitives (atoms like Button, Input, Typography), Composites (molecules like SearchBar, Card, Modal), and Patterns (organisms like Navigation, DataTable, FormWizard). Each component follows the Compound Component pattern for maximum flexibility.

Section: Fiverr Design System
A comprehensive, accessible component library powering Fiverr's marketplace UI.

Section: #Contributing
Follow the contribution guide in CONTRIBUTING.md. All components must include accessibility tests, Storybook stories, and TypeScript types.

Section: #Performance
All components are tree-shakeable with individual exports. Average component bundle size under 5KB gzipped. Storybook loads in under 3 seconds with lazy story loading.

Section: #Projects
##Fiverr Design System (Fiverr) Led development of a shared component library used by 80+ frontend engineers. Built with React, TypeScript, and Storybook. Includes 60+ accessible components with automated visual regression testing via Chromatic. Reduced UI development time by 40%.

##Real-time Collaborative Editor (Fiverr) Built a collaborative editing feature for gig descriptions using WebSockets, CRDT algorithms (Yjs), and React. Supports concurrent editing by multiple sellers with conflict resolution and presence indicators. Technologies: React, TypeScript, Yjs, WebSockets, Node.js.

##Web Performance Optimization Suite (Fiverr) Developed an internal performance monitoring and optimization toolkit. Automated Core Web Vitals tracking, bundle analysis, and performance regression detection in CI. Improved LCP by 40% and reduced bundle size by 35%.

Section: #Certifications
1. Google Professional Cloud Developer (March 2024, Google Cloud) 2. Web Accessibility Specialist (WAS) (November 2023, IAAP) 3. Meta Frontend Developer Professional Certificate (June 2022, Meta via Coursera)

Section: #Architecture
The system uses a monorepo architecture with Turborepo, organizing components into three tiers: Primitives (atoms like Button, Input, Typography), Composites (molecules like SearchBar, Card, Modal), and Patterns (organisms like Navigation, DataTable, FormWizard). Each component follows the Compound Component pattern for maximum flexibility.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Maya Chen
From: Lior Barak, Frontend Team Lead at Fiverr Date: May 25, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am delighted to recommend Maya Chen, who has been an exceptional member of our frontend engineering team at Fiverr for the past three years. Maya joined us straight from university and has rapidly become one of our most impactful engineers.

Maya's standout achievement has been leading our design system initiative. She built and maintains a component library used by 80+ engineers, with 60+ accessible components that have reduced UI development time by 40%. Her deep knowledge of accessibility standards led to our marketplace achieving full WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across 20+ product surfaces.

Her work on web performance has been equally impressive. Maya improved our Largest Contentful Paint by 40% and reduced JavaScript bundle size by 35%, directly impacting user experience for 4M+ monthly active users. She also built our real-time collaborative editor using cutting-edge CRDT algorithms.

Full WCAG 2.1 AA compliance

40% reduction in UI development time

Frontend Engineer with 3 years of experience building performant, accessible web applications at scale. Specialized in React, TypeScript, and design systems. Each component follows the Compound Component pattern for maximum flexibility.

question: Tell me about the design system project.
answer:
Maya Chen led the development of Fiverr's Design System, a comprehensive component library used by 80+ frontend engineers across 20+ product surfaces. Key aspects include:

1. **Technology Stack**: Built with React, TypeScript, and Storybook, featuring a design token system for light/dark themes and compound component patterns for composability.

2. **Accessibility**: Achieved full WCAG 2.1 AA compliance through 60+ accessible components, with automated visual regression testing via Chromatic. This ensured 20+ product surfaces met accessibility standards.

3. **Impact**: Reduced UI development time by 40% and improved web performance metrics (e.g., LCP by 40%, bundle size reduction by 35%). The system also included a reusable component library with comprehensive documentation.

4. **Collaboration**: Served as a central hub for 80+ engineers, streamlining development workflows and maintaining consistency across Fiverr's marketplace.

question: What technologies were used in the performance optimization project?
answer:
The **Web Performance Optimization Suite** project at Fiverr utilized the following technologies:

### **Frontend Tools**  
- **React** and **TypeScript** for building performant, accessible components.  
- **Chromatic** for automated visual regression testing.  
- **Webpack** for bundle analysis and optimization.  
- **Lighthouse** (via Chrome DevTools) for Core Web Vitals tracking.  

### **DevOps & Infrastructure**  
- **Docker** for containerization.  
- **GitHub Actions** for CI/CD pipelines.  
- **Vercel** and **Netlify** for deployment.  
- **AWS**:  
  - **S3** and **CloudFront** for static asset delivery.  
  - **Lambda** for serverless processing of performance data.  

### **Performance Focus**  
- **Code splitting** and **lazy loading** to reduce bundle size.  
- **Image optimization** techniques (e.g., WebP, responsive images).  
- **Tree shaking** and **dynamic imports** to minimize JavaScript payloads.  

This stack enabled a **40% improvement in LCP**, **35% reduction in bundle size**, and automated performance regression detection.

I am delighted to recommend Maya Chen, who has been an exceptional member of our frontend engineering team at Fiverr for the past three years. Maya joined us straight from university and has rapidly become one of our most impactful engineers.

Maya's standout achievement has been leading our design system initiative. She built and maintains a component library used by 80+ engineers, with 60+ accessible components that have reduced UI development time by 40%. Each component follows the Compound Component pattern for maximum flexibility.

Section: Smart Recommendation Engine (DataSphere Technologies)
Built a hybrid collaborative and content-based recommendation system serving 2M+ users. The system combines matrix factorization with deep neural networks for content understanding. Improved click-through rate by 35% compared to the previous rule-based system. Technologies: PyTorch, Redis, FastAPI, AWS SageMaker.

Section: Architecture
The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

Built a hybrid collaborative and content-based recommendation engine that improved click-through rate (CTR) by 35%.

Developed a conversational AI customer support chatbot using RAG architecture (LangChain, ChromaDB, Hugging Face Transformers), handling 70% of inbound support queries autonomously.

Section: Tech Stack
Deep Learning: PyTorch for model training and inference

Serving: FastAPI with Redis caching

Professional Scrum Master I — PSM I (June 2022, Scrum.org)

Section: Academic Capstone: Autonomous Drone Navigation
Developed a computer vision system for indoor drone navigation as a final-year capstone project at Tel Aviv University. The system uses reinforcement learning with visual inputs to navigate unknown indoor environments. Technologies: PyTorch, OpenCV, ROS (Robot Operating System). Grade: 96/100.

Section: Architecture
The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

Section: Usage
The system exposes a REST API for real-time recommendations. Configure user and item feature sources in config.yaml. Training pipelines run on SageMaker with configurable hyperparameters.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Ofir Ohan
From: Dr. Yael Stern, Director of Engineering at DataSphere Technologies Date: May 10, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to enthusiastically recommend Ofir Ohan, who has been an outstanding member of our ML engineering team at DataSphere Technologies for over four years. Ofir joined us as an ML engineer and has consistently exceeded expectations in every aspect of his work.

Ofir's most significant contribution has been the development of our Smart Recommendation Engine, which serves over 2 million users and improved click-through rates by 35%. He designed the hybrid architecture combining collaborative filtering with deep content understanding, demonstrating both research depth and engineering pragmatism. This system processes millions of requests daily with sub-50ms latency.

Section: Architecture

Deep Learning: PyTorch for model training and inference

Section: Architecture
The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

From: Dr. Yael Stern, Director of Engineering at DataSphere Technologies Date: May 10, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to enthusiastically recommend Ofir Ohan, who has been an outstanding member of our ML engineering team at DataSphere Technologies for over four years. Ofir joined us as an ML engineer and has consistently exceeded expectations in every aspect of his work.

Ofir's most significant contribution has been the development of our Smart Recommendation Engine, which serves over 2 million users and improved click-through rates by 35%. He designed the hybrid architecture combining collaborative filtering with deep content understanding, demonstrating both research depth and engineering pragmatism. The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

question: Tell me about the recommendation engine project.
answer:
The **Smart Recommendation Engine** is a hybrid system developed by Ofir Ohan at DataSphere Technologies, serving over 2 million users. It combines **collaborative filtering** with **deep content understanding** (using neural networks) to deliver personalized recommendations. Key aspects include:  

- **35% improvement in click-through rates (CTR)** compared to a previous rule-based system.  
- **Real-time inference** with sub-50ms latency via Redis caching.  
- Built on **PyTorch**, **AWS SageMaker**, **Redis**, and **FastAPI**, with distributed training capabilities.  
- Scales to handle millions of daily requests and integrates with large datasets (>50 million rows).  
- Supports **e-commerce** (product recommendations, search ranking) and **fintech** (fraud detection, risk scoring) use cases.  
- Includes an **A/B testing framework** for model comparison and **automatic retraining** on data drift detection.  

Ofir led the project, mentoring a team of engineers and ensuring production deployment of the system.

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Ofir Ohan
From: Dr. Yael Stern, Director of Engineering at DataSphere Technologies Date: May 10, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to enthusiastically recommend Ofir Ohan, who has been an outstanding member of our ML engineering team at DataSphere Technologies for over four years. Ofir joined us as an ML engineer and has consistently exceeded expectations in every aspect of his work.

Ofir's most significant contribution has been the development of our Smart Recommendation Engine, which serves over 2 million users and improved click-through rates by 35%. He designed the hybrid architecture combining collaborative filtering with deep content understanding, demonstrating both research depth and engineering pragmatism. This system processes millions of requests daily with sub-50ms latency.

Section: Architecture
The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

Section: Performance
Serving 2M+ users with sub-50ms p99 latency. Model training completes in 4 hours on 4x V100 GPUs. Daily retraining pipeline runs automatically via Airflow with drift detection.

From: Dr. Yael Stern, Director of Engineering at DataSphere Technologies Date: May 10, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to enthusiastically recommend Ofir Ohan, who has been an outstanding member of our ML engineering team at DataSphere Technologies for over four years. Ofir joined us as an ML engineer and has consistently exceeded expectations in every aspect of his work.

Section: Overview
The Smart Recommendation Engine combines matrix factorization with deep neural networks for content understanding to deliver personalized recommendations at scale. Built for DataSphere Technologies' e-commerce platform, it improved click-through rate by 35% compared to the previous rule-based system.

Section: Architecture
The system has three main components: a Collaborative Filtering module using matrix factorization (ALS) for user-item interaction patterns, a Content-Based module using a transformer-based encoder for item feature understanding, and a Hybrid Ranker that combines both signals using a learned weighting model. Real-time inference is served via FastAPI with Redis caching for sub-50ms response times.

Sincerely,

Noa Friedman — Curriculum Vitae

Section: ##SDK Integration Redesign (ironSource)
Redesigned the SDK integration experience reducing integration time from 5 days to 2 hours. Included interactive setup wizard, automated validation, and contextual documentation. Resulted in 40% increase in developer adoption.

Section: ##Real-Time Publisher Analytics Dashboard (Unity)
Designed and shipped a real-time analytics platform for 50K+ game developers to monitor ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. Features include custom segmentation, anomaly detection alerts, and revenue forecasting

Section: #Key Features
- Real-time ad revenue monitoring with 30-second refresh

- Custom segmentation by country, ad format, app version, and user cohort

- ML-powered anomaly detection with Slack/email alerts

- Revenue forecasting with confidence intervals

- Exportable reports in CSV and PDF formats

- Used by 50K+ game developers worldwide

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities.

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

Node

- Backend: js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

question: Describe the candidate's work at ironSource.
answer:
The candidate's work at ironSource included:  
- **SDK Integration Redesign**: Reduced SDK integration time from 5 days to 2 hours by redesigning onboarding processes, documentation, and introducing tools like an interactive setup wizard. This improvement drove a 40% increase in developer adoption.  
- **Self-Service Reporting Tool**: Developed a tool enabling developers to analyze ad revenue with custom dimensions and filters, enhancing transparency and usability.  
- **Bidding Waterfall Optimization**: Launched a feature increasing eCPM (effective revenue per thousand impressions) by 15% for publishers.  
- **Customer Insights**: Conducted 200+ developer interviews to refine product strategies and managed a beta program with 50 enterprise publishers.  
- **KPI & OKR Framework**: Defined metrics for the mediation team, tracking adoption, revenue, and developer satisfaction.  

These efforts underscore their expertise in product strategy, technical collaboration, and developer-centric solutions.

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities.

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

Node

- Backend: js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

Noa is an outstanding people manager. She leads a team of 3 PMs with clarity, empathy, and high standards. She established our experimentation culture, running 100+ A/B tests per quarter with rigorous statistical methodology.

I wholeheartedly recommend Noa for a Director of Product role. She combines strategic product thinking, data literacy, technical depth, and people leadership in away thatis rare and valuable

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities.

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

Node

- Backend: js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

Section: #Performance
Processes 10B+ daily ad events with sub-5-second latency for real-time metrics. Dashboard page load time under 2 seconds. Supports concurrent access thousands of publishers.

#Contributing

Intemal Unity project. Feature requests managed through Productboard

#License

Proprietary — Unity Technologies

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities.

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

Node

- Backend: js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

Section: ##Real-Time Publisher Analytics Dashboard (Unity)
Designed and shipped a real-time analytics platform for 50K+ game developers to monitor ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. Features include custom segmentation, anomaly detection alerts, and revenue forecasting

Section: #installation
Intemal Unity platform — not available for external installation.

#Usage

Publishers access the dashboard through the Unity Developer Portal. Configure custom segments, set anomaly detection thresholds, and create automated revenue reports. API access available for programmatic integration

Section: #Performance
Processes 10B+ daily ad events with sub-5-second latency for real-time metrics. Dashboard page load time under 2 seconds. Supports concurrent access thousands of publishers.

#Contributing

Intemal Unity project. Feature requests managed through Productboard

#License

Proprietary — Unity Technologies

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities.

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

Node

- Backend: js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

answer:

These projects highlight the candidate’s expertise in product leadership, data-driven decision-making, and scalable technical solutions.

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities.

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

Node

- Backend: js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

Section: #Certifications
1. Pragmatic Institute Certified Product Manager (PMC) (March 2022, Pragmatic Institute) 2. Google Data Analytics Professional Certificate (August 2021, Google via Coursera) 3. Certified Scrum Product Owner (CSPO) (May 2021, Scrum Alliance)

Section: ##ML-Powered Ad Mediation (Unity)
Led product development of a machine leaming ad mediation algorithm that optimizes waterfall configurations in real-time. Increased publisher revenue by 22% compared to manual waterfall management. Collaborated with ML team on feature engineering and model evaluation. Scale: 1.5B monthly end-users.

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

#Tech Stack

- Frontend: React, TypeScript, D3js, Recharts

Node

- Backend: js, GraphQL, gRPC

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

Section: #Overview
The Publisher Analytics Dashboard provides 50K+ game developers with real-time visibility into ad revenue, user engagement, and monetization KPIs. for Unity's monetization platform, it enables data-driven decision making with custom segmentation, anomaly detection, and revenue forecasting capabilities.

#Architecture

The platform consists of a streaming data pipeline (Kafka + Flink) for real-time event processing, a BigQuery data warehouse for historical analytics, and React-based dashboard with interactive visualizations. Real-time metrics update every 30 seconds with sub-5-second latency for critical revenue events

Node

- Data Pipeline: Apache Katka, Apache Flink

- Storage: BigQuery, Redis, PostgreSQL

- Infrastructure: GCP (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub), Kubernetes

- Monitoring: Datadog, PagerDuty

question: Describe the candidate's current job responsibilities.
answer:
The candidate's current role as **Staff Backend Engineer at AppsFlyer** involves designing and maintaining high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems for real-time mobile attribution. Key responsibilities include:

1. **System Architecture**: Designing distributed systems and event-driven architectures at scale, with a focus on reliability and performance for processing **1.2M events/second** and **100B+ daily events**.
2. **Real-Time Pipelines**: Leading the development of the **Real-Time Event Enrichment Pipeline**, which ingests raw attribution events, enriches them with device/geo/campaign metadata, applies fraud detection rules, and routes data to analytics/reporting systems.
3. **Technical Expertise**: Specializing in **Kafka-based streaming systems**, **Kafka transactions** for exactly-once processing, and **stateful sessionization** with 24-hour windows. They also implement **auto-scaling** based on consumer lag metrics and manage **multi-region active-active deployments**.
4. **Innovation**: Optimizing Raft variants for cross-datacenter replication, reducing commit latency by 40%, and prototyping in Go. Their work has been published at the ACM SOSP Workshop.
5. **Community Contributions**: Developing the open-source **KafkaFlow** project (250+ stars) to advance engineering practices and tooling.

This role emphasizes scalability, fault tolerance, and real-time processing, with direct impact on AppsFlyer's core mobile attribution platform.

answer:

- **Role**: Staff Backend Engineer (March 2020 – Present, Herzliya, Israel; September 2018 – February 2020, Ra'anana, Israel)  
- **Key Contributions**:

Section: Letter of Recommendation — Tal Ben-David
From: Reshef Mann, CTO at AppsFlyer

Date: May 22, 2026

To Whom It May Concern,

I recommend Tal Ben-David, who has been a technical cornerstone at AppsFlyer for over six years. Tal joined as a backend engineer and quickly became one of our most influential architects.

Tal's flagship achievement is the real-time event enrichment pipeline processing 1.2M events/second — the backbone of our attribution platform. He designed the architecture from scratch, solving complex challenges around exactly-once semantics, stateful processing, and multi-region consistency.

His leadership of the microservices migration — decomposing our monolith into 40+ services — transformed our engineering velocity, reducing deployment cycles from 2 weeks to 2 hours. As architecture guild leader, Tal mentors 8 senior engineers and has established design review processes that elevated our system quality.

Section: ##Real-Time Event Enrichment Pipeline (AppsFlyer)
Built a streaming pipeline processing 1.2M events/second for mobile attribution enrichment. Uses Kafka for ingestion, Flink for stateful processing, and ClickHouse for analytics. Technologies: Java, Kafka, Flink, ClickHouse, Kubernetes.

Designed active-active deployment across 3 AWS regions achieving 99.99% uptime. Includes conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs) for state synchronization and intelligent routing. Technologies: Go, Kafka, Redis, AWS.

Led decomposition of monolithic Clojure application into 40+ microservices with saga-based transactions, API gateway, and service mesh. Reduced deployment cycle from 2 weeks to 2 hours. Technologies: Kotlin, gRPC, Istio, Kubernetes.

Created an open-source Kafka consumer framework for Java/Kotlin with built-in retry, dead-letter queues, and backpressure handling. 250+ stars on GitHub. Technologies: Kotlin, Kafka.

Section: Overview
Processes 1.2M events/second for real-time mobile attribution enrichment at AppsFlyer. Ingests raw attribution events, enriches with device/geo/campaign metadata, applies fraud detection rules, and routes to analytics and reporting systems.

question: What was the outcome of the multi-region architecture project?
answer:
The multi-region architecture project, specifically the real-time event enrichment pipeline, achieved the following outcomes:  
- **High throughput**: Processed **1.2M events/second** with **P99 end-to-end latency <100ms**.  
- **Scalability**: Handled **100B+ daily events** across all pipelines.  
- **Uptime**: Achieved **99.99% uptime** with **zero data loss during region failovers**.  
- **Architecture**: Implemented an **active-active deployment across 3 AWS regions** using **Kafka MirrorMaker 2** for cross-region replication and **CRDTs (Conflict-Free Replicated Data Types)** for state synchronization.  
- **Technical Innovation**: Designed a **Raft variant** optimized for cross-datacenter replication, reducing commit latency by 40%.  

These results underpin the system's reliability and scalability, critical for AppsFlyer's attribution platform.

question: Summarize why I should consider this candidate for a principal engineer position.
answer:
**Summary of Tal Ben-David for Principal Engineer Role**  

**Technical Expertise & Leadership**:  
- **Distributed Systems Architect**: Designed high-throughput real-time pipelines (1.2M events/second) using Kafka, Flink, and ClickHouse, with active-active deployments across 3 AWS regions (99.99% uptime). Optimized Raft variants for cross-datacenter replication, reducing commit latency by 40%.  
- **Microservices Migration**: Led decomposition of a monolith into 40+ services, cutting deployment cycles from 2 weeks to 2 hours using Kotlin, gRPC, Istio, and Kubernetes.  
- **Open Source Contributions**: Developed KafkaFlow (250+ stars), a Java/Kotlin framework for robust Kafka consumers with retry, dead-letter queues, and backpressure handling.  

**Architectural Innovation**:  
- Published research on consensus protocols (MSc thesis at SOSP Workshop) and implemented conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs) for state synchronization.  
- Established design review processes and mentors 8 senior engineers, elevating system quality and team capabilities.  

**Scalability & Reliability**:  
- Built systems processing 100B+ daily events with sub-millisecond latency, ensuring platform reliability for mobile attribution.  
- Expertise in Kubernetes, Kafka, and ClickHouse for large-scale data pipelines.  

**Leadership & Impact**:  
- CTO’s recommendation highlights rare depth in distributed systems theory and pragmatic engineering excellence.  
- Proven ability to drive architectural transformations, mentor teams, and deliver mission-critical systems at scale.  

This candidate’s combination of technical mastery, leadership in complex systems, and open-source contributions makes them a strong fit for a Principal Engineer role.

question: What is this project?
answer:
The project is called the **Candidate AI Agent**, an AI-powered tool designed to act as a job candidate's digital representative. Here's a summary:

### **Purpose**  
Recruiters can interact with a secured chat interface to ask natural-language questions about a candidate's profile, skills, and experience. The agent answers accurately, drawing from the candidate's uploaded documents and a trained skill-scoring model.

### **Architecture**  
1. **Build Phase (Local/Offline):**  
   - Candidate uploads documents (CVs, certificates, etc.).  
   - The system parses, chunks, and embeds documents into a vector store (ChromaDB).  
   - A trained model estimates skill proficiency (1-5) and generates a structured JSON store of verified facts and skill scores.  

2. **Serve Phase (Hosted/Online):**  
   - A lightweight chat interface retrieves precomputed artifacts (structured store + vector index).  
   - Uses a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline for efficient, low-cost hosting without re-running heavy models.  

### **Key Features**  
- **Skill Proficiency Scoring:** A dedicated NLP model rates skills (e.g., Python, AWS) based on document evidence, avoiding keyword-only matching.  
- **Agentic Tool Chaining:** Combines `get_structured_data`, `get_skill_proficiency`, `search_documents`, and `search_project` for dynamic, context-aware responses.  
- **Synthetic Training Data:** Generates labeled candidate data for training the skill scorer, ensuring robustness against spurious patterns.  

### **Design Philosophy**  
- Separates heavy model inference (build phase) from lightweight serving (serve phase) for cost efficiency.  
- Uses open-source or API-based stacks for flexibility (local or cloud deployment).  

This system enables recruiters to quickly assess candidates beyond surface-level data, leveraging AI to surface verified, evidence-based insights.
contexts:
Section: HIGH-LEVEL ARCHITECTURE: BUILD VS SERVE
The project is split into two phases.

Build phase (run locally, occasionally): the candidate uploads documents; the system parses them, chunks them, embeds them into a vector store, and runs a trained model to estimate skill proficiency. This produces two artifacts — a ChromaDB vector index and a small JSON structured store of verified facts plus precomputed skill scores. The heavyweight models (document parsing, the skill scorer) only run here, on the candidate's own machine.

Serve phase (hosted, always on): the recruiter chat. It only reads the prebuilt artifacts and performs retrieval plus LLM calls. It deliberately avoids any heavy local model so it fits free or low-cost hosting tiers. This separation is the key design decision that makes the project cheap and simple to host.

Section: CANDIDATE AI AGENT — PROJECT OVERVIEW
This document describes the software project itself (its purpose, architecture, and engineering), so that a recruiter can ask questions about how the system was built. It contains no private data, no personal information, and no API keys or secrets.

Section: STRUCTURED STORE AND VECTOR STORE
The structured store is a small JSON file holding verified profile fields plus the precomputed skill scores and their evidence. The vector store is ChromaDB, used as a persistent on-disk index with cosine similarity. Each candidate's chunks live in their own collection; this project overview lives in a separate collection of its own, queried by the search_project tool.

Section: WHAT THE PROJECT IS
The Candidate AI Agent is an AI-powered digital representative for a job candidate. A recruiter opens a secured, always-available chat link and asks natural-language questions about the candidate; the agent answers accurately and professionally, grounded in the candidate's own documents and a trained scoring model. It goes well beyond a standard "chat with your PDF" demo: it combines an agentic ReAct-style architecture with an advanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline and a purpose-built machine-learning model for skill scoring.

The whole system can run on either a 100% local open-source stack or an all-API cloud stack, selected purely through configuration. The cloud configuration is what makes it deployable as an always-on, secured service.

Section: SYNTHETIC TRAINING DATA GENERATION
Training the skill scorer required labeled data that does not exist publicly, so the project includes a synthetic data generation system. It builds realistic candidate personas and archetypes, generates documents with controlled skill evidence at known proficiency levels, tracks phrase usage to avoid repetition, sanitizes the output, and validates the result with statistical checks, cross-validation, and shortcut-detection (to ensure the model learns from real evidence rather than spurious cues). The generator uses async LLM calls for throughput.

Section: AGENTIC TOOL CALLING (ReAct STYLE)
The system does not blindly query a vector database. An LLM agent reasons about each question and chooses among several tools, and it can chain multiple tools in one turn with fallbacks. The tools are:

get_structured_data: returns fixed, verified facts from a structured JSON store (full name, email, phone, links, education, years of experience, current and desired role, salary expectation, location, availability, work type, relocation).

get_skill_proficiency: returns the candidate's proficiency LEVEL (1 to 5) in a given skill, inferred by a trained scoring model from the candidate's documents, together with the supporting evidence. It can also list all assessed skills ranked by level.

search_documents: runs the full RAG pipeline over the candidate's unstructured documents for anything not covered by the other tools — projects, detailed experience, achievements, certifications.

Section: AGENTIC TOOL CALLING (ReAct STYLE)
search_project: answers questions about THIS software project/system itself (its architecture, technology stack, features, evaluation, and how it works), using RAG over this very overview document rather than the candidate's data.

The agent chains tools intelligently. For example, if get_skill_proficiency reports that a skill was not assessed, the agent falls back to search_documents. A safety cap limits the number of tool rounds per turn to prevent loops.

Section: SKILL PROFICIENCY ESTIMATION — THE FLAGSHIP FEATURE
Instead of leaving "how good is the candidate at X?" to keyword matching, the project trains a dedicated NLP model to score each skill from 1 to 5 directly from the candidate's own documents. The levels mean: 1 awareness (a passing mention), 2 working familiarity, 3 competent / day-to-day use, 4 strong / leads work in it, 5 expert / authority.

At setup time, for each skill the candidate lists, the system retrieves the supporting evidence chunks from their documents and runs the trained scorer. The result — a level plus the evidence it was based on — is saved into the structured store, so the recruiter chat can later surface a verified, model-grounded level quickly instead of guessing.

Section: CANDIDATE AI AGENT — PROJECT OVERVIEW
This document describes the software project itself (its purpose, architecture, and engineering), so that a recruiter can ask questions about how the system was built. It contains no private data, no personal information, and no API keys or secrets.

Section: WHAT THE PROJECT IS
The Candidate AI Agent is an AI-powered digital representative for a job candidate. A recruiter opens a secured, always-available chat link and asks natural-language questions about the candidate; the agent answers accurately and professionally, grounded in the candidate's own documents and a trained scoring model. It goes well beyond a standard "chat with your PDF" demo: it combines an agentic ReAct-style architecture with an advanced Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline and a purpose-built machine-learning model for skill scoring.

The whole system can run on either a 100% local open-source stack or an all-API cloud stack, selected purely through configuration. The cloud configuration is what makes it deployable as an always-on, secured service.

Section: STRUCTURED STORE AND VECTOR STORE
The structured store is a small JSON file holding verified profile fields plus the precomputed skill scores and their evidence. The vector store is ChromaDB, used as a persistent on-disk index with cosine similarity. Each candidate's chunks live in their own collection; this project overview lives in a separate collection of its own, queried by the search_project tool.

Section: DESIGN PRINCIPLES AND WHAT MAKES IT NOTABLE
The standout engineering choices are: a trained, evidence-grounded skill scorer instead of keyword guessing; an agent that routes between verified facts, the scorer, and document search with intelligent fallbacks; a hybrid, fused, re-ranked retrieval pipeline with a broad/specific router; a serious multi-dimensional evaluation suite including per-gate retrieval failure localization; a fully synthetic, validated training-data pipeline; and a clean build-vs-serve split with pluggable providers that lets the same codebase run fully local or deploy as a cheap, secured, always-on cloud service.

Section: THE TWO USER FLOWS
Candidate Setup page: the candidate uploads supporting documents (CV, transcripts, certificates, recommendation letters) in PDF, DOCX, TXT, MD, PNG, or JPG form, fills in structured profile fields (contact details, education, experience, job preferences), lists the skills they want assessed, and triggers skill-proficiency estimation. In production this page is hidden so recruiters can never edit the profile or trigger ingestion.

Recruiter Chat page: a clean conversational interface. The recruiter types a question; the agent decides which tools to call, gathers grounded evidence, and returns a concise professional answer in the same language the recruiter used.

Section: THE RAG PIPELINE — INGESTION
Documents are parsed with the Unstructured library (with PyMuPDF for PDFs, python-docx for Word, and OCR via Tesseract/Pillow for images). The parser performs section-aware extraction: it detects section headers (including spaced-letter headers like "T E C H N I C A L  S K I L L S") and merges consecutive elements under the same heading. Each section is then split with a recursive character text splitter (about 1000 characters per chunk with 150 characters of overlap). Every chunk is prefixed with its section name for context and stored with provenance metadata (source file and document id) so retrieved chunks can always be traced back to their origin — which is what makes retrieval evaluation against ground-truth evidence possible.

In addition to the per-chunk index, a separate summary index is built: the LLM writes a short factual summary of each document, which is embedded and stored in its own collection. This powers the "broad" retrieval path.

Section: THE EVALUATION SUITE
The project ships a rigorous, component-based evaluation harness rather than ad-hoc spot checks. It runs the full agent over golden datasets and scores several independent dimensions:

Tool Selection: did the agent pick the correct tool (structured facts vs skill scorer vs document search vs project search) for each question?

RAG Quality: RAGAS metrics (faithfulness, context precision and recall, answer relevancy) on the RAG

routed questions.

Answer Correctness: DeepEval's GEval LLM

as

of

Section: AGENTIC TOOL CALLING (ReAct STYLE)
The system does not blindly query a vector database. An LLM agent reasons about each question and chooses among several tools, and it can chain multiple tools in one turn with fallbacks. The tools are:

get_structured_data: returns fixed, verified facts from a structured JSON store (full name, email, phone, links, education, years of experience, current and desired role, salary expectation, location, availability, work type, relocation).

get_skill_proficiency: returns the candidate's proficiency LEVEL (1 to 5) in a given skill, inferred by a trained scoring model from the candidate's documents, together with the supporting evidence. It can also list all assessed skills ranked by level.

search_documents: runs the full RAG pipeline over the candidate's unstructured documents for anything not covered by the other tools — projects, detailed experience, achievements, certifications.

Section: DEPLOYMENT AND SECURITY
The recruiter chat deploys to a Streamlit-based host (such as Streamlit Community Cloud or Hugging Face Spaces) over HTTPS. Access is gated by a single shared access code suited to a single-candidate app. In production only the recruiter chat is served; the setup page is hidden. The candidate's private data is kept out of any public repository. Running costs are minimal: the all-API serve path uses a fast, inexpensive Claude model for the agent and a low-cost Voyage tier for embeddings and reranking, typically only a few dollars per month, and the hosting itself can be free.

Section: TECHNOLOGY STACK
Frontend and app framework: Streamlit (multi

page).

LLM and orchestration: Anthropic Claude API (or local Ollama), ReAct

style tool calling, prompt caching.

Embeddings: Voyage AI (or local nomic

embed via sentence

transformers).

Retrieval: ChromaDB for dense vectors, BM25 Okapi for sparse search, Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and a cross

encoder reranker (Voyage rerank or Qwen3

Reranker).

Skill scoring: DeBERTa

v3 with a CORAL ordinal head, in PyTorch / Transformers.

Ingestion: Unstructured, PyMuPDF, python

docx, Tesseract OCR, section

aware chunking via LangChain text splitters.

Evaluation: RAGAS and DeepEval (GEval), plus custom tool, router, refusal, and per

gate retrieval evaluators, with HTML/JSON/CSV reports.

Section: THE RAG PIPELINE — RETRIEVAL
Retrieval is an advanced, multi-stage pipeline:

1. Query routing. An LLM router classifies each query as BROAD (a general overview / "who is this person" question) or SPECIFIC (any particular fact, skill, project, or detail). Broad queries are answered from the summary index; specific queries go through the full fusion path.

2. Query expansion. For specific queries, the LLM generates several query variations — a mix of short keyword-style queries (good for BM25) and natural language queries (good for vector search) — to maximize recall.

3. Hybrid fusion retrieval. The system runs both dense vector search (semantic similarity over embeddings in ChromaDB) and sparse keyword search (BM25 Okapi) for every query variation, then merges all ranked lists with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF). RRF scores each list independently so rank-0 in any list gets equal weight regardless of list length or origin.

Section: PLUGGABLE PROVIDERS
This pluggability preserves the original "built entirely from local open-source models" story while enabling a lean, always-on cloud deployment.

Section: SKILL PROFICIENCY ESTIMATION — THE FLAGSHIP FEATURE
Instead of leaving "how good is the candidate at X?" to keyword matching, the project trains a dedicated NLP model to score each skill from 1 to 5 directly from the candidate's own documents. The levels mean: 1 awareness (a passing mention), 2 working familiarity, 3 competent / day-to-day use, 4 strong / leads work in it, 5 expert / authority.

At setup time, for each skill the candidate lists, the system retrieves the supporting evidence chunks from their documents and runs the trained scorer. The result — a level plus the evidence it was based on — is saved into the structured store, so the recruiter chat can later surface a verified, model-grounded level quickly instead of guessing.

Section: THE EVALUATION DATA
Evaluation uses six synthetic candidates with varied document formats (PDF, DOCX, TXT, Markdown, and image/PNG to exercise OCR). Each candidate has a golden dataset of 75 questions spanning personal facts, education, experience, preferences, skills, proficiency, projects, certifications, complex multi-tool questions, and deliberately out-of-scope "negative" questions. Project-knowledge questions about this system are evaluated the same way as the candidate document questions.

Section: PLUGGABLE PROVIDERS
Three layers are pluggable through configuration, with no code changes:

LLM: Claude via the Anthropic API (production default) or a local Ollama model. The agent loop, the query router, query expansion, and build

time summaries all go through one provider

neutral client. Prompt caching is applied to the frozen system prompt and tool definitions to reduce cost.

Embeddings: Voyage AI API (production default, no local model) or local nomic

embed

text via sentence

transformers. Both are asymmetric — documents and queries are embedded differently — so switching providers requires rebuilding the vector index.

Reranker: Voyage rerank API (production default) or a local Qwen3

Reranker. The Qwen3 reranker is a causal language model scored, per its model card, by reading the probability of the "yes" token over a query/document relevance prompt — not a naive classification head. A "none" option skips reranking.

Section: SYNTHETIC TRAINING DATA GENERATION
Training the skill scorer required labeled data that does not exist publicly, so the project includes a synthetic data generation system. It builds realistic candidate personas and archetypes, generates documents with controlled skill evidence at known proficiency levels, tracks phrase usage to avoid repetition, sanitizes the output, and validates the result with statistical checks, cross-validation, and shortcut-detection (to ensure the model learns from real evidence rather than spurious cues). The generator uses async LLM calls for throughput.

question: Give me a high-level overview of the system.
answer:
The Candidate AI Agent is a system designed to streamline recruiter-candidate interactions by leveraging AI to provide accurate, document-based insights into a candidate's qualifications. Here's a high-level overview:

1. **Core Functionality**:  
   - Automates resume screening and synthesizes structured candidate profiles (e.g., education, experience, skills) from uploaded documents.  
   - Uses a **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)** pipeline to deliver contextually grounded answers to recruiter questions.  

2. **Architecture**:  
   - **Agentic ReAct Framework**: Combines lightweight chat logic with precomputed artifacts from a local build phase.  
   - **Skill Proficiency Scoring**: A trained model estimates skill levels (1–5) for technologies/skills based on document evidence.  

3. **Tech Stack**:  
   - **Storage**: ChromaDB for vector embeddings.  
   - **Data**: JSON for structured metadata.  
   - **LLMs**: For RAG generation and agent reasoning.  

4. **Deployment**:  
   - Supports **local or cloud hosting** (e.g., secure, always-on cloud configuration).  

5. **Evaluation**:  
   - **Build Phase**: Validates document parsing, skill scoring, and RAG accuracy.  
   - **Serve Phase**: Measures chat interaction effectiveness and response quality.  

This system addresses fragmented candidate data by centralizing structured insights and skill assessments, enabling recruiters to engage with candidates via natural language queries.
contexts: The Candidate AI Agent is an AI-powered tool enabling recruiters to interact with job candidates via natural language, providing accurate, document-grounded answers about their qualifications. It solves the problem of manual resume screening and fragmented candidate data by synthesizing structured profiles and skill assessments. The system uses an agentic ReAct-style architecture with a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline, combining lightweight chat logic with precomputed artifacts from a local build phase. Its standout feature is a trained skill-proficiency scorer that estimates candidate skill levels (1–5) from uploaded documents. The tech stack includes open-source tools like ChromaDB for vector storage, JSON for structured data, and LLMs for RAG and agent reasoning, with deployment options for local or cloud hosting. Evaluation occurs during the build phase (document parsing, skill scoring) and serve phase (chat interactions), with the cloud configuration enabling secure, always-on deployment.

question: What reranker does the project use?
answer:
The project uses a **cross-encoder reranker** for re-ranking, with two pluggable options:

1. **Voyage rerank API** (production default)  
2. **Qwen3 Reranker** (local alternative)

The Qwen3 reranker is a causal language model that scores relevance by analyzing the probability of the "yes" token in a query/document relevance prompt. The Voyage rerank API is the default option in production. Both options are part of the hybrid retrieval pipeline that combines dense vector search (ChromaDB) and sparse search (BM25 Okapi) with reciprocal rank fusion.
